examples.english: I swear by the receding [planets], which fly by and fade

examples.text: أطفأتُ المكيّف وفتحت النافذة، فدخل الهواء البحريّ حارّاً وكنس الأوكسجين المُعلّب غير الصحّي من قفصي الصدري.
examples.english: I quenched the air conditioner and opened the window, and sea wind entered hot and swept the unhealthy canned oxygen out of my ribcage.

senses.gloss: plural of كَانِس (kānis, active participle of كَنَسَ (kanasa))

etymology.text: From the root ك ن س (k n s). In Fraenkel’s and Nöldeke’s view the meaning “to sweep” is from Aramaic כְּנַשׁ / ܟܢܫ (kənaš, “to gather; to sweep”), denominal via مِكْنَسَة (miknasa, “broom”). Note Mehri kənūs (“to sweep”).
